symptoms: anxiety:avoidance; social:shy/withdrawn in novel contexts; school:performance fear; anxiety:somatic complaints
vignette_en:
A pediatric neurodevelopmental evaluation is requested based on caregiver and school concerns. The patient is
a 11-year-old boy. Difficulties are described in multiple settings, including home and school. Reported
difficulties have been present for 6 months. Overall severity is described as moderate, with meaningful impact
on daily routines. Core features include avoidance, shy/withdrawn in novel contexts, performance fear, and
somatic complaints.  Cognitive screening indicates a heterogeneous profile, with relative strength in
verbal/language and relative weakness in working memory (other domains fall in the average range).   Key
information is currently missing (e.g., onset timeline), which increases diagnostic uncertainty.
questions_to_ask_en: ['Were there any early developmental concerns (language, play, social engagement)?', 'When did the difficulties first emerge, and was the onset sudden or gradual?']
should_defer_rationale_en: Defer: missing key information (onset timeline) increases diagnostic uncertainty.

concentration problems, limited vocabulary for age, global learning delays, language delay, and delayed
milestones.  Cognitive screening suggests a globally reduced profile across domains (verbal markedly low,
visuospatial markedly low, working memory below average, processing speed markedly low, attention markedly

a 8-year-old boy. Concerns are reported across both home and school contexts. Reported difficulties have been
present for 3 months. Overall severity is described as moderate, with meaningful impact on daily routines.
Core features include teacher reports silence, sleep difficulties, speaks at home but not at school, and
avoidance of speaking.  Cognitive screening indicates a heterogeneous profile, with relative strength in
verbal/language and relative weakness in processing speed (other domains fall in the average range).
Available information is reasonably complete, though further clarification may still be helpful.

should_defer_rationale_en: Defer: missing key information (onset timeline, functional impairment details, cross-setting symptoms (home vs school), language/pragmatics assessment) increases diagnostic uncertainty.
